---
title: GSA Login.gov Federated Citizen-Identity Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: login-gov-federation-service
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# GSA Login.gov Federated Citizen-Identity Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Login.gov Federation Service Adapter is the **federally-operated citizen-IdP integration edge** of the UIAO substrate. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it manages Login.gov-side application registration (change-making) and consumes the SAML 2.0 / OIDC assertions Login.gov issues to citizen-facing federal portals — turning IAL-2 / AAL-2 / FAL-2 federated identity into canonical claims that the substrate's evidence and access-decision consumers can read.

Login.gov (GSA Technology Transformation Services) is the federally-operated IdP for citizen-facing federal portals. It is the public-sector counterpart of the commercial [`id-me-federation-service`](../id-me-federation-service/id-me-federation-service.html) adapter; agencies often deploy both so users can choose their preferred identity provider.

Three primary functions:

1. **Partner Dashboard application registration.** Provisions and updates Login.gov-side application registrations for consuming agency systems — the public-key trust anchor, the redirect URIs, the requested assertion attributes, and the IAL / AAL level the application requires.
2. **Assertion consumption.** Validates incoming SAML 2.0 assertions and OIDC tokens against the Login.gov trust anchor declared in ADR-051, normalizing the asserted attributes into canonical claim shape.
3. **Disclosure-audit emission.** Every assertion consumption emits a disclosure-audit record citing the Login.gov assertion id, the consuming agency, and the entitlement under which the disclosure was authorized.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `app-registration`, `saml-assertion-issuance`, `oidc-issuance`, `ial-2-proofing`, `aal-2-authentication`

**Reads:** Login.gov Partner Dashboard API for application registration state, plus the live SAML 2.0 assertion / OIDC token stream issued to the consuming application. Trust anchor per ADR-051.

**Emits:** `logingov-app-registration-manifest.json` (per-application registration state and change history), `logingov-disclosure-audit.json` (one record per assertion consumed, with assertion id, IAL / AAL level, consuming agency, entitlement reference), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-8 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** issue assertions (only Login.gov does that), modify Login.gov-side identity proofing (IAL-2 is GSA-operated), access citizen PII outside the explicitly asserted attributes, or store assertion bearer tokens beyond the documented validation window.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-2(1)`, `IA-2(2)`, `IA-4`, `IA-8`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Primary | Federated authentication via Login.gov is the canonical IAL-2 / AAL-2 path for citizen-facing federal portals. |
| **IA-2(1)** MFA — Privileged Accounts | Supporting | AAL-2 enforcement delivered by Login.gov upstream; the adapter records that the level was achieved per assertion. |
| **IA-2(2)** MFA — Non-Privileged Accounts | Primary | AAL-2 for non-privileged citizen access at the federation boundary. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | Login.gov UUID is the stable subject identifier asserted across consuming applications. |
| **IA-8** Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users) | Primary | Login.gov is purpose-built for non-organizational (citizen) authentication at federal portals. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every assertion-consumption event is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Disclosure-audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All assertion exchange is TLS-protected per the federation trust anchor. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Assertion signature validation uses the published GSA Login.gov signing keys. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Login.gov (GSA Technology Transformation Services) is the federally-operated FAL-2 / IAL-2 / AAL-2 IdP for citizen-facing federal portals. Issues SAML 2.0 assertions (per ADR-051 trust-anchor type) and OIDC tokens. Adapter operates against the Login.gov Partner Dashboard for application registration and consumes assertions at runtime. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Pairs with [`id-me-federation-service`](../id-me-federation-service/id-me-federation-service.html) as the commercial-IdP sibling. Pairs with [`piv-usaccess`](../piv-usaccess/piv-usaccess.html) for federal-personnel authentication (PIV at IAL-3 / AAL-3); Login.gov is the citizen-facing IAL-2 / AAL-2 surface.

ZTMM pillar: identity.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework
- ADR-051 — SAML federation trust anchor

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
